revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''名蔵拝所'''（なぐらうがんじゅ）は、[[沖縄県]][[石垣市]][[石垣島]]にある拝所（うがんじゅ）である。 ==概要== 神名…'
revision.text:
'''名蔵拝所'''（なぐらうがんじゅ）は、[[沖縄県]][[石垣市]][[石垣島]]にある拝所（うがんじゅ）である。
==概要== 神名は照添照明し神。 4座の御イベ名は大本主（おもとあるじ）、東花手吉花（あがりはなてよしはな）、中大木中垂伊（なかおもとなかたらい）、袖垂大主（そでたれおおあるじ）。
かつて、名蔵に、初金、玉皿、オナリの3兄姉がいた。 初金は心がよこしまで、凶暴で、妹の諫めを聞き入れない。 あるとき、大本大主（おもとおおあるじ）が現れ、オナリに憑き、託宣した。 すなわち、こんど琉球国土の守護のために大和から3柱の姉妹神を下り、姉神は首里の弁御嶽に、2人の姉妹神は久米島に渡ったが、山が浅く俗地であるから、八重山のオモト岳の森厳な霊域に移り、諸神から大主（おおあるじ）とあおがれ、当島の守護神として島内の悪疫をはらい、農穀の豊穣をはかり、民に礼恕の徳をしめそうという。 しかし、初金はこれは偽りであるとして信じないで、本物の神であるならば、奇法妙力で山海の巨大な動物を見せてみよという。 オナリのおしえにしたがって、池に行くと、長さ7尺のイノシシに会ったから、これを拳で撃ち殺した。 また潮嶺では、長さ10尺のサメをつかみ殺した。 ますますおごって、じっさいに神を拝ませよという。 オナリはしかたなくオモト岳の頂の神の御座の中にみちびいた。 すると天神が現れ、神を偽りとして教えを信ぜず来たのだから、糠を饗しようと降らせ、初金の身を埋めて、白雲に乗って天空高く飛び去った。 初金が山を下りると、その身からは虫がわき、これに食われて半死半生の身で腹を立てて妹を打ち殺し、自らもあいはてた。 その遺体は石となって名蔵野に横たわり、オナリの遺体は神が降臨してオモト岳に召し上げられた。 玉皿はこの神の不思議を拝して大本大主をまつって人々に崇敬させたと伝える。
{{substub}} [[Category:石垣市]]

'''名蔵拝所'''（なぐらうがんじゅ）は、[[沖縄県]][[石垣市]][[石垣島]]にある拝所（うがんじゅ）である。
==概要== 神名は照添照明し神。 4座の御イベ名は大本主（おもとあるじ）、東花手吉花（あがりはなてよしはな）、中大木中垂伊（なかおもとなかたらい）、袖垂大主（そでたれおおあるじ）。
かつて、名蔵に、初金、玉皿、オナリの3兄姉がいた。 初金は心がよこしまで、凶暴で、妹の諫めを聞き入れない。 あるとき、大本大主（おもとおおあるじ）が現れ、オナリに憑き、託宣した。 すなわち、こんど琉球国土の守護のために大和から3柱の姉妹神を下り、姉神は首里の弁御嶽に、2人の姉妹神は久米島に渡ったが、山が浅く俗地であるから、八重山のオモト岳の森厳な霊域に移り、諸神から大主（おおあるじ）とあおがれ、当島の守護神として島内の悪疫をはらい、農穀の豊穣をはかり、民に礼恕の徳をしめそうという。 しかし、初金はこれは偽りであるとして信じないで、本物の神であるならば、奇法妙力で山海の巨大な動物を見せてみよという。 オナリのおしえにしたがって、池に行くと、長さ7尺のイノシシに会ったから、これを拳で撃ち殺した。 また潮嶺では、長さ10尺のサメをつかみ殺した。 ますますおごって、じっさいに神を拝ませよという。 オナリはしかたなくオモト岳の頂の神の御座の中にみちびいた。 すると天神が現れ、神を偽りとして教えを信ぜず来たのだから、糠を饗しようと降らせ、初金の身を埋めて、白雲に乗って天空高く飛び去った。
初金が山を下りると、その身からは虫がわき、これに食われて半死半生の身で腹を立てて妹を打ち殺し、自らもあいはてた。 その遺体は石となって名蔵野に横たわり、オナリの遺体は神が降臨してオモト岳に召し上げられた。 玉皿はこの神の不思議を拝して大本大主をまつって人々に崇敬させたと伝える。
[[Category:石垣市]] {{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2014年3月1日 (土) 05:01 (UTC)|ソートキー=沖縄なくらうかんしゆ}} '''名蔵拝所'''（なぐらうがんじゅ）は、[[沖縄県]][[石垣市]][[石垣島]]にある[[拝所]]（うがんじゅ）である。 {{See also|拝所}}

{{出典の明記|date=2014年3月1日 (土) 05:01 (UTC)|ソートキー=沖縄なくらうかんしゆ}} '''名蔵拝所'''（なぐらうがんじゅ）は、[[沖縄県]][[石垣市]][[石垣島]]にある[[拝所]]（うがんじゅ）である。 {{See also|拝所}}
==Overview== 神名は照添照明し神。 4座の御イベ名は大本主（おもとあるじ）、東花手吉花（あがりはなてよしはな）、中大木中垂伊（なかおもとなかたらい）、袖垂大主（そでたれおおあるじ）。
嘗て、名蔵に、初金、玉皿、オナリの3兄姉がいた。 初金は心がよこしまで、凶暴で、妹の諫めを聞き入れない。 あるとき、大本大主（おもとおおあるじ）が現れ、オナリに憑き、託宣した。 即ち、こんど琉球国土の守護のために大和から3柱の姉妹神を下り、姉神は首里の弁御嶽に、2人の姉妹神は久米島に渡ったが、山が浅く俗地であるから、八重山のオモト岳の森厳な霊域に移り、諸神から大主（おおあるじ）とあおがれ、当島の守護神として島内の悪疫をはらい、農穀の豊穣をはかり、民に礼恕の徳をしめそうという。 しかし、初金はこれは偽りであるとして信じないで、本物の神であるならば、奇法妙力で山海の巨大な動物を見せてみよという。 オナリのおしえにしたがって、池に行くと、長さ7尺のイノシシに会ったから、これを拳で撃ち殺した。 また潮嶺では、長さ10尺のサメをつかみ殺した。 ますますおごって、実際に神を拝ませよという。 オナリは仕方なくオモト岳の頂の神の御座の中に導いた。 すると天神が現れ、神を偽りとして教えを信ぜず来たのだから、糠を饗しようと降らせ、初金の身を埋めて、白雲に乗って天空高く飛び去った。
初金が山を下りると、その身からは虫がわき、これに食われて半死半生の身で腹を立てて妹を打ち殺し、自らもあい果てた。 その遺体は石となって名蔵野に横たわり、オナリの遺体は神が降臨してオモト岳に召し上げられた。 玉皿はこの神の不思議を拝して大本大主をまつって人々に崇敬させたと伝える。
{{DEFAULTSORT:なくらうかんしゆ}} [[Category:Ryukyuan religion]] [[Category:石垣市の建築物]] [[Category:Ryukyu Shinto]][[Category:new pages]] [[ja:名蔵拝所]][[Category:Ryukyu Shinto]] {{Pref-stub|pref=沖縄県}} [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:名蔵拝所]][[ja:名蔵拝所]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:untranslated pages]][[ja:名蔵拝所]][[Category:untranslated pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:名蔵拝所]][[Category:qq]]

嘗て、名蔵に、初金、玉皿、オナリの3兄姉がいた。 初金は心がよこしまで、凶暴で、妹の諫めを聞き入れない。 あるとき、大本大主（おもとおおあるじ）が現れ、オナリに憑き、託宣した。 即ち、こんど琉球国土の守護のために大和から3柱の姉妹神を下り、姉神は首里の弁御嶽に、2人の姉妹神は久米島に渡ったが、山が浅く俗地であるから、八重山のオモト岳の森厳な霊域に移り、諸神から大主（おおあるじ）とあおがれ、当島の守護神として島内の悪疫をはらい、農穀の豊穣をはかり、民に礼恕の徳をしめそうという。 しかし、初金はこれは偽りであるとして信じないで、本物の神であるならば、奇法妙力で山海の巨大な動物を見せてみよという。 オナリのおしえにしたがって、池に行くと、長さ7尺のイノシシに会ったから、これを拳で撃ち殺した。 また潮嶺では、長さ10尺のサメをつかみ殺した。 ますますおごって、実際に神を拝ませよという。 オナリは仕方なくオモト岳の頂の神の御座の中に導いた。 すると天神が現れ、神を偽りとして教えを信ぜず来たのだから、糠を饗しようと降らせ、初金の身を埋めて、白雲に乗って天空高く飛び去った。
初金が山を下りると、その身からは虫がわき、これに食われて半死半生の身で腹を立てて妹を打ち殺し、自らもあい果てた。 その遺体は石となって名蔵野に横たわり、オナリの遺体は神が降臨してオモト岳に召し上げられた。 玉皿はこの神の不思議を拝して大本大主をまつって人々に崇敬させたと伝える。

{{Source Citation|date=March 1, 2014 (Sat) 05:01 (UTC)|sort key=沖縄なくらうかんしゆ}} '''Nagura Haisho''' (名蔵拝所) is a {{ill|Worship Hall|ja|拝所}} located in {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|ja|沖縄県}}, {{ill|Ishigaki City|ja|石垣市}}, and {{ill|Ishigaki Island|ja|石垣島}}. {{See also|Worship Hall}}
==Overview== The divine name is that of the radiant god. The names of the four principal deities are '''Omoto-aruji''' (大本主), '''Agari-hanate Yoshihana''' (東花手吉花), '''Nakaomoto Nakatarai''' (中大木中垂伊), and '''Sodetare-ōaruji''' (袖垂大主).
Once, in Nagura, there were three sibling deities: '''Hatsukane''' (初金), '''Tamazara''' (玉皿), and '''Onari''' (オナリ). Hatsukane was extremely fierce and violent, and would not heed his younger sister’s admonitions. At one time, '''Omoto-ōaruji''' (大本大主) appeared, possessed Onari, and delivered an oracle. In short, for the protection of the Ryukyu nation’s territory, three sister deities descended from Yamato: the elder sister deity went to the Bengu-dake in Shuri, while the other two crossed over to Kume Island. However, since the mountains there were shallow and the land was mundane, they relocated to the solemn sacred forest of Mount Omoto in the Yaeyama Islands, where they were revered by the other deities as the chief god, destined to ward off the island’s plagues, secure abundant harvests, and bestow the virtue of benevolence upon the people. However, Hatsukane dismissed this as false, and declared that if they were truly gods, they should demonstrate miraculous power by displaying gigantic creatures of the mountains and seas. Following Onari’s instruction, he went to a pond, where he encountered a wild boar measuring 7 shaku in length, and he struck it to death with his fist. Furthermore, on the tidal ridges, he seized and killed a shark measuring 10 shaku in length. Growing even more arrogant, he demanded that they demonstrate their divinity in person. Reluctantly, Onari led him to the divine throne at the summit of Mount Omoto. Then, a heavenly deity appeared, and, regarding him as a false god for not heeding the divine teachings, caused bran to be rained down upon him, buried his body, and ascended high into the sky riding on white clouds.
When Hatsukane descended the mountain, insects began to emerge from his body; as they devoured him and left him in a state between life and death, he became enraged, struck his younger sister, and ultimately perished. His corpse turned to stone and lay in the Nagura fields, while Onari’s remains were taken up to Mount Omoto by the descent of the deity. It is said that Tamazara, in venerating the mystery of this god, came to inspire the people to revere Omoto-ōaruji.
[[Category:Ryukyuan Religion]] [[Category:Buildings in Ishigaki City]] [[Category:Ryukyu Shinto]] [[Category:New pages]] [[ja:名蔵拝所]] {{Pref-stub|pref=Okinawa Prefecture}} [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:名蔵拝所]] [[ja:名蔵拝所]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Old translated pages]] [[ja:名蔵拝所]] [[Category:Old translated pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:名蔵拝所]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]][[ja:名蔵拝所]]

{{Unreferenced|date=March 1, 2014 (Sat) 05:01 (UTC)|sort key=沖縄なくらうかんしゆ}} '''Nagura Haisho''' (名蔵拝所) is an {{ill|Haisho#Uganju|lt=Uganju|ja|拝所}} located in {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|ja|沖縄県}}, {{ill|Ishigaki City|ja|石垣市}}, and {{ill|Ishigaki Island|ja|石垣島}}.
==Overview== The divine name is that of the radiant god. The names of the four principal deities are '''Omoto-aruji''' (大本主), '''Agari-hanate Yoshihana''' (東花手吉花), '''Nakaomoto Nakatarai''' (中大木中垂伊), and '''Sodetare-ōaruji''' (袖垂大主).
Once, in Nagura, there were three sibling deities: '''Hatsukane''' (初金), '''Tamazara''' (玉皿), and '''Onari''' (オナリ). Hatsukane was extremely fierce and violent, and would not heed his younger sister’s admonitions. At one time, '''Omoto-ōaruji''' (大本大主) appeared, possessed Onari, and delivered an oracle. In short, for the protection of the Ryukyu nation’s territory, three sister deities descended from Yamato: the elder sister deity went to the Bengu-dake in Shuri, while the other two crossed over to Kume Island. However, since the mountains there were shallow and the land was mundane, they relocated to the solemn sacred forest of Mount Omoto in the Yaeyama Islands, where they were revered by the other deities as the chief god, destined to ward off the island’s plagues, secure abundant harvests, and bestow the virtue of benevolence upon the people. However, Hatsukane dismissed this as false, and declared that if they were truly gods, they should demonstrate miraculous power by displaying gigantic creatures of the mountains and seas. Following Onari’s instruction, he went to a pond, where he encountered a wild boar measuring 7 shaku in length, and he struck it to death with his fist. Furthermore, on the tidal ridges, he seized and killed a shark measuring 10 shaku in length. Growing even more arrogant, he demanded that they demonstrate their divinity in person. Reluctantly, Onari led him to the divine throne at the summit of Mount Omoto. Then, a heavenly deity appeared, and, regarding him as a false god for not heeding the divine teachings, caused bran to be rained down upon him, buried his body, and ascended high into the sky riding on white clouds.
When Hatsukane descended the mountain, insects began to emerge from his body; as they devoured him and left him in a state between life and death, he became enraged, struck his younger sister, and ultimately perished. His corpse turned to stone and lay in the Nagura fields, while Onari’s remains were taken up to Mount Omoto by the descent of the deity. It is said that Tamazara, in venerating the mystery of this god, came to inspire the people to revere Omoto-ōaruji.
[[Category:Buildings in Ishigaki City]] [[Category:Ryukyu Shinto]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:名蔵拝所]] {{Pref-stub|pref=Okinawa Prefecture}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:名蔵拝所]] [[ja:名蔵拝所]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Old translated pages]] [[ja:名蔵拝所]] [[Category:Old translated pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:名蔵拝所]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]][[ja:名蔵拝所]] {{Okinawan Shrines}}

==Overview== The divine name is that of the radiant god. The names of the four principal deities are '''Omoto-aruji''' (大本主), '''Agari-hanate Yoshihana''' (東花手吉花), '''Nakaomoto Nakatarai''' (中大木中垂伊), and '''Sodetare-ōaruji''' (袖垂大主).
Once, in Nagura, there were three sibling deities: '''Hatsukane''' (初金), '''Tamazara''' (玉皿), and '''Onari''' (オナリ). Hatsukane was extremely fierce and violent, and would not heed his younger sister’s admonitions. At one time, '''Omoto-ōaruji''' (大本大主) appeared, possessed Onari, and delivered an oracle. In short, for the protection of the Ryukyu nation’s territory, three sister deities descended from Yamato: the elder sister deity went to the Bengu-dake in Shuri, while the other two crossed over to Kume Island. However, since the mountains there were shallow and the land was mundane, they relocated to the solemn sacred forest of Mount Omoto in the Yaeyama Islands, where they were revered by the other deities as the chief god, destined to ward off the island’s plagues, secure abundant harvests, and bestow the virtue of benevolence upon the people. However, Hatsukane dismissed this as false, and declared that if they were truly gods, they should demonstrate miraculous power by displaying gigantic creatures of the mountains and seas. Following Onari’s instruction, he went to a pond, where he encountered a wild boar measuring 7 shaku in length, and he struck it to death with his fist. Furthermore, on the tidal ridges, he seized and killed a shark measuring 10 shaku in length. Growing even more arrogant, he demanded that they demonstrate their divinity in person. Reluctantly, Onari led him to the divine throne at the summit of Mount Omoto. Then, a heavenly deity appeared, and, regarding him as a false god for not heeding the divine teachings, caused bran to be rained down upon him, buried his body, and ascended high into the sky riding on white clouds.
When Hatsukane descended the mountain, insects began to emerge from his body; as they devoured him and left him in a state between life and death, he became enraged, struck his younger sister, and ultimately perished. His corpse turned to stone and lay in the Nagura fields, while Onari’s remains were taken up to Mount Omoto by the descent of the deity. It is said that Tamazara, in venerating the mystery of this god, came to inspire the people to revere Omoto-ōaruji.
[[Category:Buildings in Ishigaki City]] [[Category:Ryukyu Shinto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[ja:名蔵拝所]] {{Pref-stub|pref=Okinawa Prefecture}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:名蔵拝所]] [[ja:名蔵拝所]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Old translated pages]] [[ja:名蔵拝所]] [[Category:Old translated pages]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:名蔵拝所]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion Tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagura Uganju]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagura Uganju]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:名蔵拝所]] {{Okinawan Shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagura Uganju}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagura Uganju}} [[Category:New]]

{{Unreferenced|date=March 1, 2014 (Sat) 05:01 (UTC)|sort key=沖縄なくらうかんしゆ}} '''Nagura Haisho''' (名蔵拝所) is an {{ill|Haisho#Uganju|lt=Uganju|ja|拝所}} located in {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|ja|沖縄県}}, {{ill|Ishigaki City|ja|石垣市}}, and {{ill|Ishigaki Island|ja|石垣島}}.
==Overview== The divine name is that of the radiant god. The names of the four principal deities are '''Omoto-aruji''' (大本主), '''Agari-hanate Yoshihana''' (東花手吉花), '''Nakaomoto Nakatarai''' (中大木中垂伊), and '''Sodetare-ōaruji''' (袖垂大主).
Once, in Nagura, there were three sibling deities: '''Hatsukane''' (初金), '''Tamazara''' (玉皿), and '''Onari''' (オナリ). Hatsukane was extremely fierce and violent, and would not heed his younger sister’s admonitions. At one time, '''Omoto-ōaruji''' (大本大主) appeared, possessed Onari, and delivered an oracle. In short, for the protection of the Ryukyu nation’s territory, three sister deities descended from Yamato: the elder sister deity went to the Bengu-dake in Shuri, while the other two crossed over to Kume Island. However, since the mountains there were shallow and the land was mundane, they relocated to the solemn sacred forest of Mount Omoto in the Yaeyama Islands, where they were revered by the other deities as the chief god, destined to ward off the island’s plagues, secure abundant harvests, and bestow the virtue of benevolence upon the people. However, Hatsukane dismissed this as false, and declared that if they were truly gods, they should demonstrate miraculous power by displaying gigantic creatures of the mountains and seas. Following Onari’s instruction, he went to a pond, where he encountered a wild boar measuring 7 shaku in length, and he struck it to death with his fist. Furthermore, on the tidal ridges, he seized and killed a shark measuring 10 shaku in length. Growing even more arrogant, he demanded that they demonstrate their divinity in person. Reluctantly, Onari led him to the divine throne at the summit of Mount Omoto. Then, a heavenly deity appeared, and, regarding him as a false god for not heeding the divine teachings, caused bran to be rained down upon him, buried his body, and ascended high into the sky riding on white clouds.
When Hatsukane descended the mountain, insects began to emerge from his body; as they devoured him and left him in a state between life and death, he became enraged, struck his younger sister, and ultimately perished. His corpse turned to stone and lay in the Nagura fields, while Onari’s remains were taken up to Mount Omoto by the descent of the deity. It is said that Tamazara, in venerating the mystery of this god, came to inspire the people to revere Omoto-ōaruji.
[[Category:Buildings in Ishigaki City]] [[Category:Ryukyu Shinto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[ja:名蔵拝所]] {{Pref-stub|pref=Okinawa Prefecture}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:名蔵拝所]] [[ja:名蔵拝所]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Old translated pages]] [[ja:名蔵拝所]] [[Category:Old translated pages]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:名蔵拝所]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagura Uganju]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagura Uganju]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:名蔵拝所]] {{Okinawan Shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagura Uganju}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagura Uganju}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:500 shortest pages as of 03-29-2025]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagura Uganju]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagura Uganju]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

His corpse turned to stone and lay in the Nagura fields, while Onari’s remains were taken up to Mount Omoto by the descent of the deity. It is said that Tamazara, in venerating the mystery of this god, came to inspire the people to revere Omoto-ōaruji.

==Overview== The divine name is that of the radiant god. The names of the four principal deities are '''Omoto-aruji''' (大本主), '''Agari-hanate Yoshihana''' (東花手吉花), '''Nakaomoto Nakatarai''' (中大木中垂伊), and '''Sodetare-ōaruji''' (袖垂大主).
Once, in Nagura, there were three sibling deities: '''Hatsukane''' (初金), '''Tamazara''' (玉皿), and '''Onari''' (オナリ). Hatsukane was extremely fierce and violent, and would not heed his younger sister’s admonitions. At one time, '''Omoto-ōaruji''' (大本大主) appeared, possessed Onari, and delivered an oracle. In short, for the protection of the Ryukyu nation’s territory, three sister deities descended from Yamato: the elder sister deity went to the Bengu-dake in Shuri, while the other two crossed over to Kume Island. However, since the mountains there were shallow and the land was mundane, they relocated to the solemn sacred forest of Mount Omoto in the Yaeyama Islands, where they were revered by the other deities as the chief god, destined to ward off the island’s plagues, secure abundant harvests, and bestow the virtue of benevolence upon the people. However, Hatsukane dismissed this as false, and declared that if they were truly gods, they should demonstrate miraculous power by displaying gigantic creatures of the mountains and seas. Following Onari’s instruction, he went to a pond, where he encountered a wild boar measuring 7 shaku in length, and he struck it to death with his fist. Furthermore, on the tidal ridges, he seized and killed a shark measuring 10 shaku in length. Growing even more arrogant, he demanded that they demonstrate their divinity in person. Reluctantly, Onari led him to the divine throne at the summit of Mount Omoto. Then, a heavenly deity appeared, and, regarding him as a false god for not heeding the divine teachings, caused bran to be rained down upon him, buried his body, and ascended high into the sky riding on white clouds.
When Hatsukane descended the mountain, insects began to emerge from his body; as they devoured him and left him in a state between life and death, he became enraged, struck his younger sister, and ultimately perished. His corpse turned to stone and lay in the Nagura fields, while Onari’s remains were taken up to Mount Omoto by the descent of the deity. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:名蔵拝所]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:名蔵拝所]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:名蔵拝所]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11415550]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=March 1, 2014 (Sat) 05:01 (UTC)|sort key=沖縄なくらうかんしゆ}} '''Nagura Haisho''' (名蔵拝所) is an {{ill|Haisho#Uganju|lt=Uganju|ja|拝所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho#Uganju|qq=}} located in [[:en:Okinawa Prefecture|Okinawa Prefecture]], [[:en:Ishigaki, Okinawa|Ishigaki City]], and [[:en:Ishigaki Island|Ishigaki Island]].
==Overview== The divine name is that of the radiant god. The names of the four principal deities are '''Omoto-aruji''' (大本主), '''Agari-hanate Yoshihana''' (東花手吉花), '''Nakaomoto Nakatarai''' (中大木中垂伊), and '''Sodetare-ōaruji''' (袖垂大主).
Once, in Nagura, there were three sibling deities: '''Hatsukane''' (初金), '''Tamazara''' (玉皿), and '''Onari''' (オナリ). Hatsukane was extremely fierce and violent, and would not heed his younger sister’s admonitions. At one time, '''Omoto-ōaruji''' (大本大主) appeared, possessed Onari, and delivered an oracle. In short, for the protection of the Ryukyu nation’s territory, three sister deities descended from Yamato: the elder sister deity went to the Bengu-dake in Shuri, while the other two crossed over to Kume Island. However, since the mountains there were shallow and the land was mundane, they relocated to the solemn sacred forest of Mount Omoto in the Yaeyama Islands, where they were revered by the other deities as the chief god, destined to ward off the island’s plagues, secure abundant harvests, and bestow the virtue of benevolence upon the people. However, Hatsukane dismissed this as false, and declared that if they were truly gods, they should demonstrate miraculous power by displaying gigantic creatures of the mountains and seas. Following Onari’s instruction, he went to a pond, where he encountered a wild boar measuring 7 shaku in length, and he struck it to death with his fist. Furthermore, on the tidal ridges, he seized and killed a shark measuring 10 shaku in length. Growing even more arrogant, he demanded that they demonstrate their divinity in person. Reluctantly, Onari led him to the divine throne at the summit of Mount Omoto. Then, a heavenly deity appeared, and, regarding him as a false god for not heeding the divine teachings, caused bran to be rained down upon him, buried his body, and ascended high into the sky riding on white clouds.
When Hatsukane descended the mountain, insects began to emerge from his body; as they devoured him and left him in a state between life and death, he became enraged, struck his younger sister, and ultimately perished. His corpse turned to stone and lay in the Nagura fields, while Onari’s remains were taken up to Mount Omoto by the descent of the deity. It is said that Tamazara, in venerating the mystery of this god, came to inspire the people to revere Omoto-ōaruji.
{{Pref-stub|pref=Okinawa Prefecture}}
{{Okinawan Shrines}}
{{translated page|ja|名蔵拝所|version=50857379|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Unreferenced|date=March 1, 2014 (Sat) 05:01 (UTC)|sort key=沖縄なくらうかんしゆ}} '''Nagura Haisho''' (名蔵拝所) is an {{ill|Haisho#Uganju|lt=Uganju|ja|拝所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho#Uganju|qq=}} located in {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|WD=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ishigaki, Okinawa|en|Ishigaki, Okinawa|WD=Q838366|lt=Ishigaki City}}, and {{ill|Ishigaki Island|en|Ishigaki Island|WD=Q714339|lt=Ishigaki Island}}.
==Overview== The divine name is that of the radiant god. The names of the four principal deities are '''Omoto-aruji''' (大本主), '''Agari-hanate Yoshihana''' (東花手吉花), '''Nakaomoto Nakatarai''' (中大木中垂伊), and '''Sodetare-ōaruji''' (袖垂大主).
Once, in Nagura, there were three sibling deities: '''Hatsukane''' (初金), '''Tamazara''' (玉皿), and '''Onari''' (オナリ). Hatsukane was extremely fierce and violent, and would not heed his younger sister’s admonitions. At one time, '''Omoto-ōaruji''' (大本大主) appeared, possessed Onari, and delivered an oracle. In short, for the protection of the Ryukyu nation’s territory, three sister deities descended from Yamato: the elder sister deity went to the Bengu-dake in Shuri, while the other two crossed over to Kume Island. However, since the mountains there were shallow and the land was mundane, they relocated to the solemn sacred forest of Mount Omoto in the Yaeyama Islands, where they were revered by the other deities as the chief god, destined to ward off the island’s plagues, secure abundant harvests, and bestow the virtue of benevolence upon the people. However, Hatsukane dismissed this as false, and declared that if they were truly gods, they should demonstrate miraculous power by displaying gigantic creatures of the mountains and seas. Following Onari’s instruction, he went to a pond, where he encountered a wild boar measuring 7 shaku in length, and he struck it to death with his fist. Furthermore, on the tidal ridges, he seized and killed a shark measuring 10 shaku in length. Growing even more arrogant, he demanded that they demonstrate their divinity in person. Reluctantly, Onari led him to the divine throne at the summit of Mount Omoto. Then, a heavenly deity appeared, and, regarding him as a false god for not heeding the divine teachings, caused bran to be rained down upon him, buried his body, and ascended high into the sky riding on white clouds.
When Hatsukane descended the mountain, insects began to emerge from his body; as they devoured him and left him in a state between life and death, he became enraged, struck his younger sister, and ultimately perished. His corpse turned to stone and lay in the Nagura fields, while Onari’s remains were taken up to Mount Omoto by the descent of the deity. It is said that Tamazara, in venerating the mystery of this god, came to inspire the people to revere Omoto-ōaruji.
{{Pref-stub|pref=Okinawa Prefecture}}
{{Okinawan Shrines}}
{{translated page|ja|名蔵拝所|version=50857379|comment=Imported full ja history}}
